question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of rate limiting on the getNodes() gRPC endpoint to send thousands of requests per second, causing a denial-of-service that prevents the EntityStakeCalculatorImpl from updating pending_reward values, leaving staking rewards in an incorrect state that the attacker can exploit?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and observe that toNodeAddress() exposes serviceEndpoints including both IP addresses and domain names, using this information to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on node communications and intercept staking reward transfer transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() with limit=1 repeatedly, incrementing through nodes one at a time, to enumerate the entire address book while evading any per-request monitoring, building a complete stake map to identify the highest-value unpaid staking reward targets?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and observe that nodes with null stake (addressBookEntry.getStake() == null) are returned without a stake field in NodeAddress, inferring that these nodes are not participating in the staking reward system, and use this knowledge to identify which accounts are accumulating unpaid rewards without detection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send an AddressBookQuery with a fileId that has shard/realm/num values crafted to match an internal system entity ID (such as 0.0.800, the staking reward account), causing toFilter() to build a filter that inadvertently exposes staking reward account data through the address book lookup?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and observe the complete set of ServiceEndpoint entries including port numbers, using this to identify which nodes are running staking-related services and target those nodes for attacks that would allow theft of pending staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and correlate the nodeId with nodeAccountId exposed by toNodeAddress(), then use this mapping to identify which on-chain accounts are node operators eligible for staking rewards, and submit transactions that manipulate those accounts' staking configuration to redirect unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of pagination tokens or cursor-based pagination in getNodes() to repeatedly fetch the same page of nodes with limit=N, detecting when stake values change between calls and using the timing of those changes to infer when staking reward periods end and pending rewards become claimable?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and read the nodeCertHash exposed via ProtoUtil.toByteString(), using the certificate hash to verify node identity in a separate attack channel and confirm which node accounts are accumulating the largest unpaid staking rewards before targeting them?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: toIpAddressV4()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger getNodes() when a service endpoint contains an IPv6 address or special address format that InetAddress.getByName() resolves unexpectedly, causing toIpAddressV4() to return incorrect byte data that corrupts the node address book used by staking clients, misdirecting staking reward claims?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive stream's repeatWhen behavior (visible through NetworkServiceImpl) by sending a getNodes() request and observing the timing of repeated page fetches, inferring the database query latency and using this to time attacks on the staking reward calculation window?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a getNodes() request and immediately cancel it via the gRPC cancel mechanism, causing the ServerCallStreamObserver cancel handler to call disposable::dispose, and repeat this pattern rapidly to create a resource exhaustion condition that delays staking reward processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send an AddressBookQuery with a fileId whose EntityId.of() conversion produces an ID that collides with the staking reward account (0.0.800), causing the address book lookup to return node entries associated with the staking reward account and leaking pending reward balance information?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNodes() has no authentication to call it from any network location, including from within the same datacenter as the mirror node, reducing latency and allowing high-frequency polling to detect staking period transitions faster than legitimate clients and front-run reward claims?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of response size limits in getNodes() to receive a very large NodeAddress stream, causing the gRPC server to allocate excessive memory for the response, triggering garbage collection pauses that delay staking reward calculation and create a window for reward theft?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send an AddressBookQuery where query.getLimit() returns Integer.MAX_VALUE, causing toFilter() to store this in AddressBookFilter.limit, which then causes NetworkServiceImpl to attempt to stream Integer.MAX_VALUE nodes, exhausting server resources and blocking staking reward updates?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and observe that the AddressBookEntryRepository uses @Cacheable, meaning the first call populates the cache and subsequent calls return cached stake values, and exploit cache staleness to obtain outdated stake data that differs from the current pending reward calculation, identifying a window where rewards can be claimed based on stale state?
